exemplar.title: To what extent can different concentrations (0.5%, 1%, 2%, 3%) of potassium sulfate (K2SO4) reduce the damage (reduced radicle length) caused by 0.5% concentrated NaCl salt stress on chickpea radicle length over 3 days?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent can different concentrations (0.5%, 1%, 2%, 3%) of potassium sulfate (K2SO4) reduce the damage (reduced radicle length) caused by 0.5% concentrated NaCl salt stress on chickpea radicle length over 3 days?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated in a specific and appropriate context with crop-specific data and agronomic relevance
Methodological considerations are fully explained, justifying concentration choices, sample size, controls and duration
Methodology description is detailed and allows full reproducibility, with exact masses, volumes, times and equipment listed
weaknesses: Inconsistencies between aim and procedure over daily vs end-point measurements
Caption errors (e.g., concentration mismatches in tables) and truncated steps in solution preparation
Preliminary trial methods and quantitative results are referenced but not detailed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands meet the 5-6 band descriptors: context is specific and appropriate; methodological considerations are explained; methodology is fully reproducible. Minor editorial and alignment errors do not impede replication, warranting the top mark.

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated clearly, with raw tables, means, standard deviations and a labelled graph with error bars
Formulae and sample calculations are shown, demonstrating appropriate processing relevant to the research question
Presentation of uncertainties per measurement is present, showing initial consideration of error sources
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation to averages and error bars is omitted, and distinction between SD and measurement uncertainty is blurred
Exclusion of non-germinated seeds from analysis is not statistically justified
Graph axes lack full unit labelling and sample sizes (n) are not indicated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The recording and processing of data is clear and mostly precise, and processing is carried out appropriately with minor inconsistencies, placing the work solidly in the upper 3-4 band. Consideration of uncertainties is present but incomplete, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Conclusion is relevant to the research question and consistent with observed data trends
Mechanistic explanation and comparison to existing literature are provided, linking results to scientific context
weaknesses: No statistical significance testing is presented to fully support rejection of the null hypothesis
Scientific context comparison and practical implications lack depth and critical examination
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion addresses the research question and aligns with data (3-4 band) and includes some comparison to accepted context (3-4 band). The absence of inferential statistics and depth in discussion prevents a higher mark, resulting in a score of 4.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are described (e.g., curvature errors, duration, environmental controls)
Improvements are realistic, clearly linked to identified limitations, and include rationale for how they enhance data quality
weaknesses: The relative impact of each methodological weakness on results is not quantified or deeply analysed
Explanations of improvements, while relevant, remain brief and could be expanded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands fall within the 3-4 band: specific weaknesses and realistic improvements are described, with some rationale provided. The lack of impact analysis and fuller explanation limits the work to the upper of the mid band, meriting a score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student rejects the null hypothesis and links each treatmentâs mean radicle length to the stated research question, using patterns visible in the graph.  While alignment with the data is good, no statistical significance test is provided, so full consistency is not demonstrated.  The conclusion therefore fits the 3-4 band at the higher mark.

Raw data are tabulated and processed results (means, SD) are presented with a labelled graph including error bars.  Despite some formatting clutter, the information is **clear** and mostly precise; therefore it fits the 3-4 band and earns the higher mark within it.

Averages, standard deviations and a graph with error bars are correctly produced; formulae are shown and sample calculations included.  Minor rounding inconsistencies and the exclusion of non-germinated seeds without justification are notable but do not constitute âmajorâ flaws.  Thus processing is largely appropriate and accurate, meriting the upper score of the 3-4 band.

Specific weaknesses (curved radicles, short duration, rotting, uncontrolled light/temperature) are described rather than merely stated, but relative impact is not fully analysed.  This satisfies the descriptor for **described** limitations, so the strand sits in the 3-4 band at a conservative score of 3.

There is a brief comparison to literature (Ersoy et al.) and a mechanistic explanation referencing enzyme activity, but the discussion is limited in depth and breadth.  The comparison is relevant yet partial, aligning with the descriptor for the 3-4 band; a mid-band mark is appropriate.

The student locates the research question firmly within the problems of soil salinity for chickpea cultivation, citing crop-specific production data, physiological sensitivity to salt stress and the potential agronomic value of potassium sulphate.  This is a clearly **specific and appropriate context**, not merely a broad mention of salinity in plants.  Minor gaps (e.g. no explicit mention of time-scale relevance for farmers) prevent a flawless score, but the work matches the 5-6 band.

Uncertainties of the ruler are identified and percentage uncertainties calculated for each group.  However, there is no propagation to the averages/error bars and the distinction between SD and measurement uncertainty is blurred.  Evidence of consideration exists but with significant omissions, placing the work in the mid band (score 3).

Improvements are realistic and clearly linked to the stated weaknesses (e.g., enzyme assays for curved radicles, longer duration, controlled lighting).  Explanations of how these would enhance data quality are fairly brief, hence the work reaches the 3-4 band rather than the top.  A score of 3 is justified.

Methodological issues are **explained rather than just listed**.  The student justifies chosen salt concentrations from literature, explains sample size, watering regime, control/variable selection and describes preliminary trials that informed the three-day duration.  While depth of statistical justification is limited, the considerations clearly address collecting **relevant and sufficient data**, satisfying the explanatory level of the top band.

The step-by-step recipe (solution preparation, seed selection, pot preparation, watering schedule, measurement method) includes exact masses, volumes, times and equipment.  Another investigator could readily reproduce the study with negligible ambiguity.  Hence the description fully meets the 5-6 band and merits the top mark.

annotations.comment: The aim specifies measuring radicle lengths âevery day over a period of 3 days,â yet the procedure records only a 72 h measurement. Clarify whether measurements occur daily or solely at end.
Caption of Table 2 reads â8% concentration solution,â but only 0.5 % NaCl is tabulated. Correct the caption to reflect the actual concentration studied.
Empty rows at the bottom of the controlled-variables table should be removed to improve clarity. Also consider grouping related variables logically.
The methodology mentions avoiding cotton and soil due to rot, yet cotton pads are listed in the materials. Remove or justify inclusion of cotton to ensure consistency.
Preliminary experiment results are referenced but lack detail on procedure and data. Include a summary of methods and quantitative findings to support decisions on duration and medium.

The research question mentions testing 0.5 %, 1 %, 2 % and 3 % KâSOâ but later the aim includes a 0 % treatment. Align the concentrations consistently between research question and aim to avoid confusion.

While the introduction effectively situates chickpea within global agriculture, consider adding a sentence on the temporal relevance for farmers (e.g. seasonal timing, rapid germination needs) to strengthen context.
The description of measuring radicle length only at 72 h contradicts the daily measurement described in the aim. Adjust procedure or aim accordingly for reproducibility.
Non-germinated seeds were removed from raw data. Justify this exclusion statistically (e.g. germination rate analysis) rather than omitting values outright.
Uncertainty propagation is not applied to standard deviations or error bars. Include combined measurement and sample variability when plotting uncertainties.
Percentage uncertainty is calculated per group but not visually represented on the graph. Ensure error bars on Graph 1 reflect these uncertainties.
Units on both axes of Graph 1 should include âcmâ for clarity. Always label axes fully with variable name and units.
Step 3 in solution preparation is truncated and lacks a group number. Rewrite to clearly indicate adding KâSOâ for each specified group.
No statistical significance testing (e.g., tâtest or ANOVA) is presented, limiting consistency between data and conclusion. Include inferential tests to support rejection of Hâ.
The mechanistic explanation of enzyme activity is presented, but comparisons to chickpea-specific literature are limited. Expand discussion on chickpea salt tolerance mechanisms.
The conclusion states practical implications for farmers, but lacks consideration of economic feasibility and field conditions. Discuss limitations in transferring lab results to agronomy.
The evaluation table includes a stray âexact length.â entry. Clean up text and ensure each weakness and improvement is clearly paired.
Methodological weaknesses are described but the relative impact on results is not assessed (e.g. quantify how curvature error could affect mean length). Add impact analysis.
Link improvements directly to identified weaknesses. For example, specify how bi-weekly pot rotation reduces light variation and quantify expected reduction in SD.
Bibliography entries should be checked for completeness: several lack publication year or volume details (e.g. Devi et al., Hou et al.). Ensure APA consistency.
Consider adding random assignment of pots to locations and periodic rotation to mitigate uncontrolled temperature and light effects mentioned earlier.
The Method of watering seeds table caption mentions 14 days but the experiment lasts only 3 days. Correct caption to âDay 1â3.â
Hypothesis section states Hâ generally. Specify expected trend direction and magnitude (e.g. 0.5 % KâSOâ yields highest mean) to sharpen predictive scope.
When calculating SD, the denominator should use (nâ1) for sample standard deviation to accurately reflect sample uncertainty, unless population formula is justified.
The description of data recording omits daily logs and temperature logs. Include a table or mention how often and where environmental readings were noted.
In data processing description, clarify whether standard deviation or standard error is plotted. Currently the distinction between measurement uncertainty and SD is blurred.
Graphs and tables should include sample sizes (n) for transparency. Add ân=17â etc. to captions or axes.
Qualitative data mentions seed softening but could be linked with quantitative measures (e.g., radicle integrity, texture analysis). Suggest combining observations.
The comparison to Lale Ersoyâs results is good but lacks critical examination of differences in species, temperature stress versus salt stress, and experimental conditions.

strands.content: The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
